name: Kinshasa
notes: Capital city-province. Retained the name Kinshasa throughout the Zaire era. Never renamed unlike other provinces.

name: Bandundu
notes: Retained the name Bandundu during the Zaire era. One of the provinces that kept its name through all regime changes.

name: Bas-Zaïre
notes: Formerly Bas-Congo, renamed Bas-Zaïre under Mobutu's authenticité policy in 1971. Reverted to Bas-Congo in 1997 after Mobutu's fall, then became Kongo Central in 2015.

name: Équateur
notes: Retained the name Équateur during the Zaire era. Mobutu's home province — received significant development investment. Split into 5 provinces in 2015.

name: Kasaï-Occidental
notes: Retained the name Kasaï-Occidental through the Zaire era. Rich in diamond deposits.

name: Kasaï-Oriental
notes: Retained the name Kasaï-Oriental through the Zaire era. Mbuji-Mayi became a major diamond mining center under Mobutu.

name: Shaba
notes: Formerly Katanga, renamed Shaba (meaning 'copper' in Swahili) under Mobutu's authenticité policy in 1971. Site of two Shaba invasions (1977, 1978). Reverted to Katanga in 1997.

name: Haut-Zaïre
notes: Formerly Province Orientale, renamed Haut-Zaïre under Mobutu's authenticité policy in 1971. Reverted to Orientale in 1997. Scene of major battles during the Simba Rebellion (1964) and both Congo Wars.

name: Maniema
notes: Split from Kivu province in 1988 along with Nord-Kivu and Sud-Kivu. Named after the Maniema region, historically associated with the Arab-Swahili slave and ivory trade.

name: Nord-Kivu
notes: Split from Kivu province in 1988. Home to Virunga National Park and the mountain gorillas. Later became epicenter of the First and Second Congo Wars and the ongoing M23 conflict.

name: Sud-Kivu
notes: Split from Kivu province in 1988. Borders Rwanda and Burundi. Significantly affected by the Rwandan genocide spillover and subsequent Congo Wars.
